learning_outcomes.description: Examine alternative facade solutions for a framed building.
Analyse and detail appropriate facade materials and construction systems.
Collaboratively prepare an environmental performance analysis report.
Independently prepare a set of technical drawings.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

The project context for all three assignments will be the analysis and redesign of a proposed commercial building situated on a site in Brisbane. Your client has placed an option to purchase a site with a developed building proposal but before finalising the deal, they want you to assess the design of the building; specifically, the building's performance and the wellbeing of its future users. The client is a fledgling company interested in a holistic approach to low carbon futures encompassing energy efficiency, human comfort and wellbeing, and innovative low carbon construction. For the first stage, you have been commissioned to undertake a due diligence report on the proposed building design that they have their option on. If your findings reveal shortcomings in the building design, then they will ask you to develop design proposals to improve the performance of the building against certain metrics. This second stage is split into assignments two and three. The client will use all this information to help shape their offer before closing the deal on the site.

In the first stage, working as a team you will prepare an expanded due diligence report with topics such as: analysis of building design compliance with Building codes and relevant Australian Standards; environmental site analysis including building orientation, solar gain, overshadowing, effects of wind and adjacent noise; identify local transport and services connections; articulate embodied energy and energy use; investigate alternative building frames; refer to best building design in precedents located both locally and globally.

As part of this assessment, an introduction to group work will occur in week 1, and your group will be required to complete a group work task plan, clearly outlining the expected tasks of each group member, and the timeline for which the work is to be completed. It is also a requirement of the assessment that a peer evaluation be completed separately by each individual group member.

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

Having reviewed your analysis of the building design proposal for the site, the client has decided to move onto the next stage, asking you to prepare some considered concepts that will help make the building design better.

This second stage shall be worked up individually using the report developed with your team. The work shall be developed to a sketch design level of detail, to be explained in class. The proposal shall be produced as an A3 report with an accompanying 5-minute presentation providing an overview of the proposal, highlighting its main elements for improvement. These may include a key response to the environmental analysis, opportunities for a different type of building frame, designing a higher performing façade that demonstrates improved performance, or reconfigure planning layouts for increased user comfort.

Presentations are an important way for articulating your individual proposals conceptually to tutors, before developing and detailing them for the final assignment. In each student presentation, tutors will ask questions about the proposal.

The oral presentation is worth 10% of the course grade. The submitted report itself is not graded but should reflect the amount of work in progress that has been completed and the level of understanding of the project by individual students.

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

The client is pleased with the progress on the proposals; they have now asked you to develop the detail of the façade and frame so that they can pass on the information to their quantity surveyor to get a better idea of cost before finalising their option on the design and site. The client has asked you to provide some detailed drawings that will be useful to their preferred façade supplier who will be G James Glass and Aluminium. The set-out and details of drawings required shall be workshopped in class. Drawings range from: diagrams to explain design strategies; elevations of the building; detail elevation, plan and section showing glass specification, frame configuration and relationship with building frame; and one visualisation of the building in context that illustrates the key message of the project.

Attendance in class workshop tutorials include progress check-ins with tutors, to encourage development of your proposal and address any queries or concerns you may have about assignment tasks.

This assessment includes activity in which a student’s individual performance is directly monitored; the oral presentation (Assignment 2). The oral presentation is worth 10% of the mark for Assignment 3. In order to pass this course students must achieve a minimum grade of 50% (a grade of 4) for Assignment 3.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
